methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Vital Lyfe is a Seed-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Jonathan Criss and Andrew Harner, having raised $24M to date. The Climate & Energy sector comprises 9 peers in the private dataset and 1 public comparable (LNZA), though only 1 public and 8 private firms are represented across the broader frontier-tech landscape. The peer set includes established names such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen, though none provided sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage data density to support a Peer-Median valuation method.

As a result, valuation relies on Stage-Progression methodology. Using the $24M total raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, this method implies a $60M post-money valuation. This approach is grounded in observed step-up patterns but carries inherent model risk given the absence of narrow peer multiples to validate the 2.5x step-up coefficient against Climate & Energy–specific dynamics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, centered at $60M. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier for Vital Lyfe's specific subsector within Climate & Energy, the limited peer-comp density at Seed stage, and the absence of revenue or unit-economics data to ground relative valuation. A narrower, more defensible range will require either sector-specific comparable multiples or disclosure of company-specific financial metrics.
